2604.09229.title: The Fast Lane Hypothesis: Von Economo Neurons Implement a Biological Speed-Accuracy Tradeoff
2604.09229.paper_summary: Von Economo neurons (VENs) are large bipolar projection neurons found exclusively in the anterior cingulate cortex (ACC) and frontal insula of species with complex social cognition, including humans, great apes, and cetaceans. Their selective depletion in frontotemporal dementia (FTD) and altered development in autism implicate them in rapid social decision-making, yet no computational model of VEN function has previously existed. We introduce the Fast Lane Hypothesis: VENs implement a biological speed-accuracy tradeoff (SAT) by providing a sparse, fast projection pathway that enables rapid social decisions at the cost of deliberate processing accuracy. We model VENs as fast leaky integrate-and-fire (LIF) neurons with membrane time constant 5 ms and sparse dendritic fan-in of eight afferents, compared to 20 ms and eighty afferents for standard pyramidal neurons, within a spiking cortical circuit of 2,000 neurons trained on a social discrimination task. Networks are evaluated under three clinically motivated conditions across 10 independent random seeds: typical (2% VENs), autism-like (0.4% VENs), and FTD-like (post-training VEN ablation). All configurations achieve equivalent asymptotic classification accuracy (99.4%), consistent with the prediction that VENs modulate decision speed rather than representational capacity. Temporal analysis confirms that VENs produce median first-spike latencies 4 ms earlier than pyramidal neurons. At a fixed decision threshold, the typical condition is significantly faster than FTD-like (t=-23.31, p<0.0001), while autism-like is intermediate (mean RT=26.91+/-9.01 ms vs. typical 20.70+/-2.02 ms; p=0.078). A preliminary evolutionary analysis shows qualitative correspondence between model-optimal VEN fraction and the primate phylogenetic gradient. To our knowledge, this is the first computational model that asks what a Von Economo neuron actually computes.
2604.09229.paper_summary_zh: Von Economo 神經元 (VENs) 是一種大型雙極性投射神經元，僅存在於具有複雜社會認知的物種的前扣帶皮質 (ACC) 和額葉島，包括人類、大猩猩和鯨類。<lb/>它們在額顳癡呆症 (FTD) 中的選擇性耗竭以及在自閉症中的發展變化，使它們與快速社會決策相關聯，但之前並不存在 VEN 功能的計算模型。<lb/>我們提出了快速通道假說：VENs 通過提供稀疏、快速的投射通路來實現生物學上的速度-準確性權衡 (SAT)，這使得快速社會決策的代價是深思熟慮的處理準確性。<lb/>我們將 VENs 模型化為快速漏積-發火 (LIF) 神經元，膜時間常數為 5 毫秒，稀疏的樹突進入為八個傳入神經元，相較於標準錐體神經元的 20 毫秒和八十個傳入神經元，這是在一個包含 2,000 個神經元的尖峰皮質電路中訓練於社會辨別任務。<lb/>在 10 個獨立隨機種子下，網絡在三個臨床動機的條件下進行評估：典型 (2% VENs)、類自閉症 (0.4% VENs) 和類 FTD (訓練後 VEN 切除)。<lb/>所有配置達到相當的漸近分類準確率 (99.4%)，這與 VENs 調節決策速度而非表徵能力的預測一致。<lb/>時間分析確認 VENs 產生的中位首次尖峰潛伏期比錐體神經元早 4 毫秒。<lb/>在固定的決策閾值下，典型條件顯著快於類 FTD (t=-23.31, p<0.0001)，而類自閉症則居中 (平均反應時間=26.91+/-9.01 毫秒對比典型的 20.70+/-2.02 毫秒; p=0.078)。<lb/>初步的進化分析顯示模型最佳 VEN 比例與靈長類動物的系統發育梯度之間存在定性對應。<lb/>據我們所知，這是第一個計算模型，詢問 Von Economo 神經元實際計算了什麼。
2604.09229.author: Esila Keskin et.al.
